notes: Parameter names mirror sequence.components.optical_channel.QuantumChannel (distance comes from the topology edge, not this model). Literature source: Kucera et al., Demonstration of quantum network protocols over a 14-km urban fiber link, arXiv:2404.04958, reports bare 1550-nm fiber attenuation in the 0.19-0.23 dB/km range and a PDL-derived polarization-process fidelity lower bound F_P >= 0.991; this profile uses the representative 0.2 dB/km value converted to 0.0002 dB/m and maps the lower-bound process fidelity to SeQUeNCe's scalar polarization_fidelity. Wu et al., SeQUeNCe, arXiv:2009.12000, uses c*=2e8 m/s for fiber propagation, converted here to 0.0002 m/ps. The frequency field is a simulator scheduling cap rather than a fiber-material measurement; it is pinned to the current SeQUeNCe QuantumChannel default of 8e7 Hz so resolved configs are explicit and reproducible.
